test: Fetch calls from a non-github.com browser origin, verified in headless Chrome

findings.browserResult: OK — response readable from foreign origin
TypeError: Failed to fetch — blocked by browser CORS enforcement

findings.browserResultInferred: same as device/code — no Access-Control-Allow-Origin header on response

conclusion: design.md's 'browser-only app with no server, no proxy' claim for device flow is false as measured. api.github.com is fully CORS-enabled; the two github.com login endpoints are not.
resolution: Introduce a minimal secretless CORS relay (e.g. Cloudflare Worker) that forwards only these two POST requests verbatim and adds a CORS header to the response. Verified locally with wrangler dev + headless Chrome: relay call returned a real device_code/user_code (200 OK) from a foreign origin where the direct call failed.

deployedOriginConfirmation.note: Rerun of the same three-call check from the actual deployed origin (real browser DevTools console, not headless/local), against the real deployed relay (not wrangler dev).

results.directDeviceCode: TypeError: Failed to fetch — CORS-blocked, same as local finding
results.apiGithubComDirect: 401 Bad credentials (dummy token) — reached GitHub directly, not CORS-blocked

deployedOriginConfirmation.conclusion: Confirms the local finding and its fix both hold from the real deployed Pages origin and a really-deployed (not locally-run) relay.
